definition: Automated decision-making and profiling tools that produce decisions with legal/significant effects.
AI systems that generate text, images, audio, or video (LLMs, image generators, voice synthesis).
orrick_signal: User-Facing AI; Frontier or General-Purpose AI

definition: AI systems specifically producing synthetic media with deception/harm risk (deepfakes, CSAM generators, voice clones).
orrick_signal: AI CSAM; AI Intimate Images; AI Deepfakes; AI Likeness

definition: AI systems processing biometric data including facial recognition, emotion detection, or biometric-based identification.
Algorithmic content recommendation and curation systems (social media feeds, news, advertising).
orrick_signal: AI in Social Media & Online Platforms

definition: AI systems used to set prices, rates, or terms dynamically.
Predictive models used in consequential decisions (credit scoring, predictive policing, insurance rating).
Foundation models or general-purpose AI systems with broad capabilities not restricted to one use case.
orrick_signal: Frontier or General-Purpose AI; Comprehensive AI

definition: Laws applying to any AI system without a specific system-type restriction.
